description: This bird was sitting with a few other Cackling Geese (at least a couple of which were typical Richardson's Cacklers) at the edge of an enormous Snow Goose flock. The bird stood out due to its dark bronzy chest. It was a bit larger than the nearby nominate Cackling Geese. Its bill was relatively long and slender. The forehead was steeper than that of taverneri Cackling Geese, but not as abrupt as typical of nominate Cacklers. The forehead curved smoothly into a relatively flat crown. The rear crown was rounded. This head shape was held throughout our observation, with the bird sometimes at rest, sometime alert; the shape was typical in my experience for Aleutian Cackling Goose (B.h. leucopareia). The cheek patch was narrow and unindented. The bird had a moderate black gular stripe. A hint of white feathering could be seen at base of black neck when neck stretched out. Otherwise, the bird had the share characters of white-cheeked geese, see photos.
